description: Fine-grained earth that can be molded when wet and hardened by heat; the shaping earth. 'Mueri' names what yields to hands—earth willing to become vessel.
sound_symbolism: The word molds yielding. 'Mu' grounds with earthy mass, clay gathered. The vowel 'e' yields to pressure. Then 'ri' shapes with directing hands.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo mueri' (the clay). Raw material for 'tomi' (pot). Transforms through fire into ceramic.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Clay became vessel—the ancient transformation of earth into storage, cooking, art.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Malleability as beauty—clay accepts any form, inviting organic shapes and flowing lines.
